entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_mode
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

receivedPermissions.description: together (quorum across the group tree) sign a merkle root that authorizes arbitrary (chain, contract, data) operations to be executed through this multisig.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_cronos
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

receivedPermissions.description: together (quorum across the group tree) sign a merkle root that authorizes arbitrary (chain, contract, data) operations to be executed through this multisig.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_4348158687435793198
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

Owner can rotate OCR signers/transmitters, change dynamic config, and change rate limits.

entries.name: CCIPBypasserMCMS
entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: cancel any pending scheduled operation, effectively veto-ing proposed changes.
execute any call through the timelock with no delay, bypassing the review window entirely.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_hashkey
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_2_from_blast
entries.description: v1.2 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain it serves. Still active for sources whose lane has not been migrated to v1.5. A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Owner can rotate OCR signers/transmitters, change dynamic config, and change rate limits.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_scroll
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_6
entries.description: v1.6 CCIP inbound ramp — a single per-chain contract that serves every source (as opposed to the per-source OffRamp in v1.5). Combines the v1.5 CommitStore (blessed merkle roots) and OffRamp (message execution) into one. Uses MultiOCR3: two separate Chainlink OCR instances, one per plugin (0=Commit, 1=Execution). Commit OCR signs source-chain merkle roots; Execute OCR signs lists of messages to deliver with their proofs. RMN blesses commit roots, and once blessed anyone can submit proofs to execute individual messages. Owner can rotate OCR signers/transmitters per plugin, change dynamic config, and enable/disable RMN verification per source chain.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: FeeQuoter, message interceptor, permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution becomes permissionless), isRMNVerificationDisabled (escape hatch).

commitOcrSigners.description: OCR DON signer set for COMMIT reports (plugin 0). F+1 of these addresses must co-sign each source-chain merkle root before this OffRamp stores it. Compromising the quorum allows forging roots; RMN blessing is the second line of defence.

commitOcrTransmitters.description: OCR transmitters for COMMIT reports (plugin 0). Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this OffRamp on-chain.

commitOcrF.description: Fault tolerance for the COMMIT plugin. At most F signers may be faulty; F+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be stored.

commitOcrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator for COMMIT-plugin signatures. Changes on each setOCR3Config for plugin 0.

executeOcrSigners.description: OCR DON signer set for EXECUTION reports (plugin 1). In the v1.6 design this list is typically empty: execution is authorised only by a valid merkle proof against an already-committed (and RMN-blessed) root, so no signature quorum is required per execute report. If this list is non-empty, F+1 of these addresses must additionally co-sign every execution report.

executeOcrTransmitters.description: OCR transmitters for EXECUTION reports (plugin 1). Only these addresses can submit execution reports to this OffRamp on-chain. In the v1.6 permissionless-execution design this is the primary gate on who can drive message delivery; merkle proofs do the authorisation.

executeOcrF.description: Fault tolerance for the EXECUTION plugin. At most F signers may be faulty; F+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

executeOcrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator for EXECUTION-plugin signatures. Changes on each setOCR3Config for plugin 1.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters per plugin (which rotates who attests commit roots and who orders execution), change dynamic config, and disable RMN verification per source chain.

entries.name: CCIPRateLimitAdminMCMS
entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

entries.name: FeeQuoter
entries.description: CCIP v1.6 FeeQuoter (replacement for PriceRegistry in v1.5). Stores per-token fee configuration, per-destination-chain gas costs, and the price feeds that update both. Used by the v1.6 OnRamp to compute fees in fee-token (LINK, WETH, native) for any cross-chain message. Owner can change supported fee tokens, set per-token fee config, and rotate authorized callers / price updaters.

getFeeTokens.description: ERC20 tokens accepted as fee payment when sending CCIP messages.

getAuthorizedCallers.description: Addresses permitted to push price updates into this FeeQuoter (the v1.6 OffRamp / OCR transmitters).

owner.description: Address that can change supported fee tokens, fee config, and authorized callers.

receivedPermissions.description: together (quorum across the group tree) sign a merkle root that authorizes arbitrary (chain, contract, data) operations to be executed through this multisig.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_fraxtal
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_2_from_wemix
entries.description: v1.2 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.2 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_metis
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_2_from_metis

Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_7222032299962346917
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: ARMProxy
entries.description: Immutable proxy for the CCIP Risk Management Network (RMN). Forwards blessing/cursing calls to the active ARM implementation (getARM). Its owner can atomically swap the underlying ARM implementation, effectively rotating the entire risk-validation system with no delay at the proxy level (any delay comes from whatever contract holds the owner role).

getARM.description: Current RMN (ARM) implementation. Swapped by the owner to rotate risk validation.

owner.description: Address that can swap the ARM implementation.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_linea
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

entries.name: CCIPTimelock
entries.description: OpenZeppelin AccessControl-based TimelockController. PROPOSER_ROLE schedules arbitrary (target, value, calldata) operations. After minDelay passes, EXECUTOR_ROLE can execute them. CANCELLER_ROLE can cancel pending operations before execution. BYPASSER_ROLE can execute arbitrary operations without any delay, which is the weakest link in the timelock guarantee. ADMIN_ROLE can grant and revoke any of the above roles.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: grant or revoke PROPOSER / EXECUTOR / CANCELLER / BYPASSER / ADMIN role to any address.
grant or revoke PROPOSER / EXECUTOR / CANCELLER / BYPASSER / ADMIN role to any address.

accessControl.description: Snapshot of every AccessControl role and its current members, derived from RoleGranted / RoleRevoked events.

proposers.description: Members of PROPOSER_ROLE. Can schedule any (target, value, calldata) operation to execute after minDelay.

executors.description: Members of EXECUTOR_ROLE. Can execute operations after their minDelay has elapsed.

cancellers.description: Members of CANCELLER_ROLE. Can cancel any operation that has been scheduled but not yet executed.

bypassers.description: Members of BYPASSER_ROLE. Can execute arbitrary operations WITHOUT waiting for the minDelay. This is the weakest link in the delay guarantee.

admins.description: Members of ADMIN_ROLE. Can grant or revoke any role on this timelock (except DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE which is unused).

getMinDelay.description: Delay (in seconds) between when PROPOSER_ROLE schedules an operation and when EXECUTOR_ROLE can execute it.

entries.name: RegistryModuleOwnerCustom
entries.description: Self-service onboarding hook for the TokenAdminRegistry. Anyone can call registerAdminViaOwner(token) or registerAdminViaGetCCIPAdmin(token) here to nominate themselves as the CCIP admin for an ERC20 token they control (proven via owner() or getCCIPAdmin() on the token). The nomination is forwarded to the TokenAdminRegistry, where the caller becomes the administrator who can then call setPool to bind a TokenPool to the token.

entries.name: RMNExecutorProxy
entries.description: Minimal immutable forwarder that anyone can call to invoke a target with calldata. When granted EXECUTOR_ROLE on a Timelock, it makes timelocked operations publicly executable by any caller once the delay has elapsed (no privileged keeper needed). Grants no delay-skipping power; only serves as the public execution pathway after the delay.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: execute any scheduled operation once its delay has elapsed.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_zircuit
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_bitlayer
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: LinkToken
entries.description: Chainlink's LINK ERC20 on Ethereum. Not part of CCIP; held by CCIP contracts as a fee token. Leaf — its admin chain is outside of CCIP's trust surface.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_blast
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: RMNTimelock
entries.description: OpenZeppelin AccessControl-based TimelockController. PROPOSER_ROLE schedules arbitrary (target, value, calldata) operations. After minDelay passes, EXECUTOR_ROLE can execute them. CANCELLER_ROLE can cancel pending operations before execution. BYPASSER_ROLE can execute arbitrary operations without any delay, which is the weakest link in the timelock guarantee. ADMIN_ROLE can grant and revoke any of the above roles.

accessControl.description: Snapshot of every AccessControl role and its current members, derived from RoleGranted / RoleRevoked events.

proposers.description: Members of PROPOSER_ROLE. Can schedule any (target, value, calldata) operation to execute after minDelay.

executors.description: Members of EXECUTOR_ROLE. Can execute operations after their minDelay has elapsed.

cancellers.description: Members of CANCELLER_ROLE. Can cancel any operation that has been scheduled but not yet executed.

bypassers.description: Members of BYPASSER_ROLE. Can execute arbitrary operations WITHOUT waiting for the minDelay. This is the weakest link in the delay guarantee.

admins.description: Members of ADMIN_ROLE. Can grant or revoke any role on this timelock (except DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE which is unused).

getMinDelay.description: Delay (in seconds) between when PROPOSER_ROLE schedules an operation and when EXECUTOR_ROLE can execute it.

entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_base
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_bitlayer
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: RMNProposerMCMS
entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: schedule arbitrary calls from this timelock for delayed execution.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_hedera
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_corn
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: Router
entries.description: User-facing CCIP entry point. ccipSend routes outbound messages to the destination-chain OnRamp registered for the destination chain selector. Inbound messages are delivered by OffRamps via routeMessage. Owner can replace the OnRamp for any destination (outbound attack surface) and add or remove OffRamps (inbound attack surface). The wrappedNative field names the WETH-like token users pay native fees in.

onRamps.description: destinationChainSelector → OnRamp address. One OnRamp per destination (swapped when a lane is upgraded).

offRamps.description: List of (sourceChainSelector, offRamp) the Router accepts inbound messages from. An attacker who adds a malicious OffRamp can deliver arbitrary messages to any receiver as if they came from CCIP.

getArmProxy.description: RMN proxy consulted implicitly through OnRamp/OffRamp. Not queried by Router directly.

getWrappedNative.description: Wrapped native (WETH). Users sending CCIP messages can pay fees in native via this contract.

owner.description: Address that can replace any OnRamp, add or remove OffRamps, and change wrappedNative.

entries.name: RMNCancellerMCMS
entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: cancel any pending scheduled operation, effectively veto-ing proposed changes.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_lens
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: CCIPExecutorProxy
entries.description: Minimal immutable forwarder that anyone can call to invoke a target with calldata. When granted EXECUTOR_ROLE on a Timelock, it makes timelocked operations publicly executable by any caller once the delay has elapsed (no privileged keeper needed). Grants no delay-skipping power; only serves as the public execution pathway after the delay.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: execute any scheduled operation once its delay has elapsed.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_cronoszkevm
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: RMNBypasserMCMS
entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: execute any call through the timelock with no delay, bypassing the review window entirely.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

entries.name: PriceRegistry
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP price registry. Holds the current USD price of each fee-token and the current gas price for each supported destination chain. v1.5 OnRamps query it to compute fees; v1.5 CommitStores push fresh prices into it. Owner can change the set of fee tokens and the set of price-updaters (addresses permitted to push prices).

getFeeTokens.description: ERC20 tokens accepted as fee payment for v1.5 CCIP messages.

getPriceUpdaters.description: Addresses permitted to push fee-token USD prices and destination gas prices. Typically the v1.5 CommitStores (via OCR transmitters).

owner.description: Address that can change supported fee tokens and authorized price updaters.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_6
entries.description: v1.6 CCIP outbound ramp — a single per-chain contract that serves every destination (as opposed to the per-destination OnRamp in v1.5). Destination is identified by destChainSelector in the emitted CCIPMessageSent event. Uses the FeeQuoter for fee computation, delegates nonce tracking to the NonceManager, and consults the RMN. Owner can swap the dynamic config (FeeQuoter pointer, fee aggregator, allowlist admin, message interceptor) and per-destination configuration.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable: rmnRemote (risk validator), nonceManager, tokenAdminRegistry.

staticConfigChains.description: Local chain selector.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, update per-destination configs, and pause the ramp.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_mind
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_7222032299962346917
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: CCIPCancellerMCMS
entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: cancel any pending scheduled operation, effectively veto-ing proposed changes.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_avalanche
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: TokenAdminRegistry
entries.description: Registry that maps each ERC20 token to its CCIP TokenPool. Every v1.5+ OnRamp / OffRamp consults this mapping to find the pool for an attached token. Each registered token has its own administrator (distinct from this contract's owner) who can call setPool to redirect the pool for that token, which effectively controls minting/releasing behavior for cross-chain transfers of that token. This contract's owner can appoint or revoke administrators and toggle whether outsiders can self-register tokens.

poolsMap.description: token → TokenPool mapping. One pool is the current authorized handler for each registered token's cross-chain transfers. Values are not BFS-walked (the per-token pool set is outside the shared-infra scope).

pools.description: Flat list of currently-registered TokenPool addresses (values of poolsMap).

owner.description: Address that can appoint/revoke per-token administrators and toggle self-registration.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_wemix

entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: cancel any pending scheduled operation, effectively veto-ing proposed changes.
schedule arbitrary calls from this timelock for delayed execution.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_avalanche
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_xlayer
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector, onRamp (source-chain OnRamp, not walkable), armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_metal
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: CCIPProposerMCMS_A
entries.description: Chainlink's merkle-root-based multisig (MCMS). Signers are grouped into up to NUM_GROUPS hierarchical groups; each group has a quorum (groupQuorums) and a parent (groupParents) forming a tree. A merkle root of (chain, contract, data) operations is valid once the root of the group tree is signed. Then anyone can execute individual ops under that root via setRoot / execute.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: cancel any pending scheduled operation, effectively veto-ing proposed changes.
schedule arbitrary calls from this timelock for delayed execution.

NUM_GROUPS.description: Maximum number of signer groups (32, capacity limit).

MAX_NUM_SIGNERS.description: Maximum number of signers the contract can hold (200). Capacity limit, not a trust parameter.

getConfig.description: Current signer roster. Contains signers (addr, index, group), groupQuorums (how many signatures each group needs), and groupParents (tree structure). A root is valid when every non-empty group has its quorum met in the subtree.

signers.description: Flat list of MCMS signer EOAs. A quorum of these signers across the group tree constitutes the authority this multisig controls.

getRoot.description: Current signed merkle root. validUntil is the UNIX timestamp after which the root expires and a new signed root is required to act.

getOpCount.description: Monotonic counter of operations executed under any past root. Each root carries [preOpCount, postOpCount]; ops outside that range cannot be executed with it.

receivedPermissions.description: together (quorum across the group tree) sign a merkle root that authorizes arbitrary (chain, contract, data) operations to be executed through this multisig.
together (quorum across the group tree) sign a merkle root that authorizes arbitrary (chain, contract, data) operations to be executed through this multisig.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_opbnb
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

entries.name: RMNRemote
entries.description: CCIP v1.6 Risk Management Network on this chain. Holds the curse state (isCursed, getCursedSubjects) that halts CCIP messaging, and the signer set (getVersionedConfig.signers) whose fSign-of-N threshold blesses commit reports off-ramps will execute. A cursed subject (global or per-source-chain) causes the v1.6 OffRamp to refuse execution for any message under that subject. Owner can rotate signers and change the signing threshold.

isCursed.description: Whether ANY subject on this chain is currently cursed. If true, CCIP v1.6 OffRamp refuses to execute messages.

getCursedSubjects.description: Subjects currently cursed. A cursed 'global' subject halts ALL inbound messaging; a per-source curse halts only that lane.

getLocalChainSelector.description: CCIP chain selector for Ethereum. Immutable; if wrong, no lanes resolve.

getReportDigestHeader.description: Domain separator mixed into RMN signed digests. Ensures signatures bound to CCIP v1.6 RMN cannot be replayed elsewhere.

getVersionedConfig.description: Current RMN signer set and fSign-of-N threshold. fSign signatures over a commit digest are sufficient to bless a commit root that OffRamps will execute.

owner.description: Address that can rotate the RMN signer set and change the signing threshold.
