entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_mode
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_zora
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

priceRegistry.description: PriceRegistry that this CommitStore pushes fee-token price updates to (pulled from the dynamic config).

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: priceRegistry. Owner can point to a new PriceRegistry.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every commit report this CommitStore will store. Compromising the quorum allows forging merkle roots that the paired OffRamp would then execute; their silence halts new commits.

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed commit report to this CommitStore on-chain. A transmitter with a valid signed report from f+1 signers can cause the CommitStore to store the root.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for a commit report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every commit-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config; watch-mode picks up config rotations through this.

owner.description: Address that can rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who validates messages on this lane), change the priceRegistry, and pause.

entries.name: CommitStore_v1_5_from_apechain
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound-lane commit root store — one per source chain, paired 1:1 with a v1.5 OffRamp. A Chainlink OCR DON posts signed commit reports (merkle root of messages sent on the source chain + sequence range + token-price updates) here. Once ARM has blessed the root, the paired OffRamp accepts merkle proofs against it to execute messages. Owner can rotate the OCR committee (f, signers, transmitters, configDigest), change the dynamic config, and pause.

entries.name: OffRamp_v1_5_from_monad
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP inbound ramp — one instance per source chain (sometimes multiple per source while old versions are being decommissioned). A Chainlink OCR DON signs execution reports (a list of messages to execute + their merkle proofs); a transmitter submits the report on-chain; the OffRamp verifies each message's proof against the commit root in its paired CommitStore, consults RMN for curse state, and delivers the message to the Router. Tokens are released/minted via the per-token TokenPool. Owner can change the dynamic config and rate limits.

commitStore.description: Paired CommitStore that holds the blessed merkle commit roots this OffRamp verifies proofs against.

getStaticConfig.description: Immutable: chainSelector (local), sourceChainSelector (source), onRamp (source-chain OnRamp address, not walkable from this chain), commitStore, prevOffRamp, armProxy.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable: permissionLessExecutionThresholdSeconds (how long before manual execution is permissionless), router, priceRegistry, messageInterceptor. Owner can change at any time with no ramp-level delay.

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane inbound rate limits.

ocrSigners.description: Oracle DON signer set for execution reports. f+1 of these addresses must co-sign every execution report this OffRamp will process; the report bundles N messages and their merkle proofs against the paired CommitStore's commit root. A compromised quorum can reorder, duplicate, or omit message executions (but cannot forge unknown messages without a bad commit root).

ocrTransmitters.description: Oracle transmitters. Only these addresses can submit a signed execution report to this OffRamp on-chain.

ocrF.description: Fault tolerance. The protocol assumes at most f of the OCR signers are faulty or dishonest; f+1 signatures are required for an execution report to be accepted.

ocrConfigDigest.description: Domain-separator that signers mix into every execution-report signature. Changes on each setOCR2Config.

owner.description: Address that can change the dynamic config, rotate OCR signers/transmitters (which rotates who decides execution order and liveness on this lane), change the router pointer, and pause the ramp.

receivedPermissions.description: together (quorum across the group tree) sign a merkle root that authorizes arbitrary (chain, contract, data) operations to be executed through this multisig.

entries.name: OnRamp_v1_5_to_opbnb
entries.description: v1.5 CCIP outbound ramp — one instance per destination chain. Takes ccipSend calls from the Router, computes fees via the PriceRegistry, collects them in the fee token, locks/burns any attached token amounts via their TokenPool, and emits CCIPSendRequested. Owner can change the dynamic config (fee tokens, gas limits, message interceptor) and disable the ramp. Funds do NOT sit here: token custody is in per-token TokenPools; fees in the fee aggregator.

staticConfigAddresses.description: Immutable (set at construction): prevOnRamp is the previous OnRamp for this lane (historical); rmnProxy is the RMN proxy consulted before routing a send.

staticConfigChains.description: Local (Ethereum) chainSelector and the destChainSelector this OnRamp targets.

getDynamicConfig.description: Mutable config: fee-token pricing, default gas limit, message interceptor, priceRegistry. Owner can update at any time with no delay at the ramp level (delay comes from the Timelock that owns this ramp).

getTokenLimitAdmin.description: Admin that can change per-lane aggregate rate limits (total value throughput per time window).

getFeeAggregator.description: Address that receives accumulated fee tokens from this OnRamp. Owner can change.

owner.description: Address that can replace the dynamic config, pause the ramp, change fee aggregator, and rotate the rate limit admin.
